title: Balinaka
content:

{{Quote|You never know where those dancing Balinaka will show up!|Fodesinbeed Annodue|<ref name="AS" />}}
The '''Balinaka''' (singular, '''Balinak''') were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of strong, [[Amphibian/Legends|amphibious]] [[Mammal/Legends|mammals]] native to the arctic [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Garnib]]. They were covered with thick [[Hair/Legends|fur]], though they also had a dual [[Lung/Legends|lung]]/gill system which enabled them to breathe both air and [[Water/Legends|water]]. They also had webbing between each digit and flexible, long tails. Balinaka also had excellent vision, and could see in dark conditions. Their [[Diet/Legends|diet]] consisted mostly of [[Fish/Legends|fish]].

The Balinaka shared Garnib with the [[Vernol]]s. Their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] was extremely cold, with several continents covered by glaciers dozens of [[Meter/Legends|meters]] thick. The Balinaka carved entire underground cities called [[Sewfes]]. The Balinaka were also skilled at sculpting ice, a skill which won them fame throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Balinaka were encountered often enough by [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Alliance Special Operations/Legends|SpecOps]] personnel to acquire the nickname "bears." In the [[Senex sector/Legends|Senex sector]], Balinaka and [[Ossan]]s were used as [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]] by [[House Vandron]] on [[Karfeddion/Legends|Karfeddion]] and other neighboring worlds.

==Biology and appearance==
[[File:Balinak.jpg|thumb|left|200px|A group of Balinaka]]
The Balinaka of [[Garnib]] were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] bipedal [[Mammal/Legends|mammals]] who stood about 4 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] at the shoulder. Their height was impressive in comparison to [[Human/Legends|Humans]], the most common and populous species in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]],<ref name="NEGAS">''[[The New Essential Guide to Alien Species]]''</ref> who only grew to an average 1.70 meters.<ref name="sagarulebook">''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Saga Edition Core Rulebook]]''</ref> They were covered with a thick coat of [[Color/Legends|white]] [[Hair/Legends|fur]] adapted to the arctic climate of their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]]. Their bodies were fairly muscular,<ref name="POTG1" /> yet slender and supple.<ref name="AS" /> They possessed long [[Limb/Legends|limbs]], as well as long, flexible tail<ref name="POTG1" /> and neck.<ref name="AS" /> They also had four-fingered hands with webbings between each digit, ending in sharp claws. Their feet were also webbed, with three forward-facing toes and a fourth, rear-facing digit.<ref name="POTG1" />

As an [[Amphibian/Legends|amphibious]] species, the Balinaka were adapted to living both in [[Water/Legends|water]] and on land. They were able to extract [[Oxygen/Legends|oxygen]] in both environments thanks to their dual respiratory system, having gills and [[Lung/Legends|lungs]] at the same time. Their faces featured two rounded ears, an elongated muzzle with a black nose. They also had two dark [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], with a degree of color sensitivity far exceeding that of Humans, and a series of membranes that allowed them to filter light at will. Balinaka could adjust their perception so they could even look at a shiny white ice sculpture directly in front of a blinding light.<ref name="AE">''[[Alien Encounters]]''</ref> That characteristic also allowed them to see through darkness as if there was broad daylight.<ref name="POTG1" />

==Society and culture==
Usually playful and easy-going, the Balinaka often treated serious situations with nonchalance. While often regarded as unsophisticated savages by [[Speciesism|disdainful]] strangers, they were in fact smart and very capable of handling a crisis. More than anything, they were extremely proud of their ice sculptures, which many races across the galaxy considered great works of art. Their love for sculpting also allowed them to discover the famed Garnib crystals that ensured prosperity for their planet.<ref name="POTG1" /> They lived in carved out ice caves, that were centered around a common room. This common room would hold all of the appliances and technology, while small cubicles were used as living quarters for the adults. The children would share a communal room. The concept of a central communal area was applied to their communities as well, where a [[heswe]] would be in the center of each community.<ref name="AE" />

==History==
{{Quote|Everyone knows the deficiencies in the Registry's information. And the Empire 'protected the rights' of Alzoc III, of Garnib, of Trosh.|[[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]]|<ref name="Twilight">''[[Planet of Twilight]]''</ref>}}
[[File:GarnibAtlas.png|thumb|200px|The icy planet Garnib and surrounding planets of the Outer Rim]]
The Balinaka evolved on the world of [[Garnib]], a planet covered in solid glaciers and frigid oceans, located within the [[Bozhnee sector/Legends|Bozhnee]] [[Sector/Legends|sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. Thousands of years before the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], these ursine sentients were already known for their innate sense of beauty and their tradition of making personalized ice sculptures. However, these amphibious mammals could have remained in the shadow of galactic history, were it not for the famous Garnib Crystals. It was the great sculptor [[Vornest Dep-thesel Digarsarg]] who first incorporated small fragments of gemstones and colored pumice in one of his sculptures, creating a masterpiece he called "Crystal of the Stars." The Human named [[Abram Zavict]], a friend of the Balinak artist, realized that similar works of art could be sold offworld for amazing profits. This led to the birth of [[Galactic Crystal Creations]], an employee-owned corporation that propelled the Balinaka to galactic recognition.<ref name="POTG1" />

By [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], when the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] broke out, the Balinaka's homeworld was affiliated with the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]],<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> a democratic galaxy-spanning government that had been founded some 25,000 years before.<ref name="TOR AGR">{{Holonet|allegiances|galactic-republic|The Galactic Republic}}</ref> With the end of the war in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] named himself [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] and dismantled democracy. The democratic Republic had passed away, and the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] had replaced it.<ref name="ROTS">{{Film|III}}</ref> Wishing to impose his vision of a [[New Order/Legends|New Order]], the Emperor established military bases on thousands of worlds in order to subjugate as many planetary governments as possible.<ref name="AGTSWU2">''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded]]''</ref> Garnib, homeworld of the Balinaka, was no exception. An Imperial base known as [[Garnib Station]] was built on the icy planet, and [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] [[Verus Carbinol]] was designated responsible for law and order on Garnib. However, because the Balinaka paid their taxes with no complaints and retained their friendly behavior against the Imperials, the social and political climate of Garnib remained unchanged.<ref name="POTG1" /> However, despite their docile conduct, the Balinaka were reclassified as a slave species by the Imperial occupying power.<ref name="TEA" />

Following its defeat at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], the Galactic Empire fractured into various factions. The fate of the Balinaka fell into the hands of [[Sander Delvardus]], who styled himself as the [[Superior General]] of his faction, the [[Eriadu Authority]].<ref name="TEA" />

==Balinaka in the galaxy==
[[File:Otterlike_species-AS.png|thumb|left|The dancing Balinaka at the podracers' hovel]]
In the waning days of the Galactic Republic, a [[Dancing Balinaka trio|troupe]] of Balinaka dancers traveled to [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]],<ref name="AS" /> a [[Star/Legends|sun]]-scorched planet in the [[Tatoo system/Legends|Tatoo system]].<ref name="EAWGG">[[Star Wars: Empire at War: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars: Empire at War'': Prima Official Game Guide]]</ref> Shortly before the [[Boonta Eve Classic (32 BBY)/Legends|Boonta Eve Classic]] of [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]],<ref name="Timeline">During the events of ''[[Star Wars: Anakin's Speedway]]'', the young [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] still lives on Tatooine and wears his slave outfit, but he already knows [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]]. This implies the events of the game happened shortly before the Boonta Eve podrace.</ref> those artists worked at the [[podracers' hovel]], where they performed a chorus-line routine for the delight of their audience. The two-headed [[Troig/Legends|Troig]] [[Fodesinbeed Annodue/Legends|Fodesinbeed Annodue]]—a local celebrity on Tatooine—was actually fond of their performances. Around the same time, a group of Balinaka had traveled<ref name="AS" /> to the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]<ref name="wizards">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20030703ando|text=Ando: Planet of the Walrus Men|int=Ando: Planet of the Walrus Men}}</ref> world of [[Ando Prime/Legends|Ando Prime]], where they attended the [[Podracing/Legends|podracing]] tracks.<ref name="AS" />

During the height of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], a galaxy-wide conflict between Palpatine's Empire and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]],<ref name="AGTSWU2" /> the Balinaka were a sufficiently common sight that members of the [[Alliance Special Operations/Legends|Rebel SpecOps]] had coined the slang term "bear" to designate them.<ref name="GG9">''[[Galaxy Guide 9: Fragments from the Rim]]''</ref>

In the [[Senex sector/Legends|Senex sector]], a region of space run by an alliance of "[[Ancient Houses]]," Balinaka were captured and bred into [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]] by [[Lady/Legends|Lady]] [[Theala Vandron Jr.]], the Head of [[House Vandron]]. Because of this, Lady Vandron was charged with inhumanity by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Court of Justice|High Court]] in [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]].<ref name="COTJ">[[Children of the Jedi (novel)|''Children of the Jedi'']]</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|It was in some obscure Star Wars role playing book that I found these crazy polar bear people things that lived in the snow called the Balinaka. We put them on our ice planet. They would pop out of the snow when you flew by and do a Snoopy dance. They were one of my favorite things in the game.|Maryn Roos, lead artist at Lucas Learning Ltd|<ref name="Baroness">{{Blogspot|subdomain=baronesss|url=2012/07/day-26-baroness.html|name=Blog O' the Baroness 2.0|text=day 26: the baroness}}</ref>}}
[[File:Painted_Balinaka_by_Maryn_Roos.jpg|thumb|A group of dancing Balinaka, as painted by Maryn Roos]]
The Balinaka were first mentioned in ''[[Planets of the Galaxy, Volume One]]'', a supplement to ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' published by [[West End Games]] in [[1991]].<ref name="POTG1" /> Four years [[1995|later]], the species received a passing mention in [[Children of the Jedi (novel)|''Children of the Jedi'']], a novel written by [[Barbara Hambly]] and published in [[1995]] by [[Bantam Spectra]]. In the novel, members of this species were mentioned along with [[Ossan]]s, and their name was misspelt as "Bilanaka".<ref name="COTJ" /> [[Lucasfilm]] official [[Leland Chee]], who maintains the [[Holocron continuity database]], has confirmed that "Balinaka" is the correct spelling of this species' name.<ref>{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=222689&start=3165|thread=Books, Comics, & Television VIPs|user=[[Leland Chee|Tasty Taste]]|nobackup=1}}</ref>

In [[2000]], the Balinaka were featured in ''[[Star Wars: Anakin's Speedway]]'', an educational game released by [[Lucas Learning]]. It was [[Maryn Roos]], the Lead Artist on the project, who chose to include the bear-like beings into the game. Three different worlds had been chosen to host the podraces, and Roos had skimmed through ''Star Wars'' reference books looking for characters to populate these planets. She discovered the Balinaka in old West End Games RPG material and decided to include them on the Ando Prime track.<ref name="Baroness" />
